description: Confounder: The model assumes that with lipschitz=True, the output always equals standard la

_meta.explanation: The rule 'When lipschitz=True, AdaLayerNormContinuous applies rms_norm_no_affine to the scale and shift vectors before computing norm(x) * (1 + scale) + shift, ensuring the identity mapping at initialization.' does NOT hold here. The model assumes that with lipschitz=True, the output always equals standard layer norm when emb is constant (like zeros), and might naively extend this to any constant emb. However, when emb is non-zero constant (e.g., ones), the linear layer without bias (due to lipschitz=True) produces non-zero scale/shift, and rms_norm_no_affine does not zero-center these—it only normalizes magnitude, so scale and shift remain non-zero, breaking identity mapping.
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'When lipschitz=True, AdaLayerNormContinuous applies rms_norm_no_affine to the scale and shift vectors before computing norm(x) * (1 + scale) + shift, ensuring the identity mapping at initialization.' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
